reviews.comment: Just the best

Great Professor who cared about teaching the students the learning material, no matter how simple it was! The only participation was for answering questions at the start of class, but he also gave out textbooks to anyone who was willing to question (My term). Super fun presentation; even used props. Hopefully he teaches more courses in the future😊
Talks a lot about future stuffs in course, but quite nice man

NICE PROF!!! He is so good I would like to say that. The most challenging parts of his lectures are that they are really sleepy and he will call random students to answer questions before class started. But just to check attendance, and even try to provide you the answer by super clear hints. Anyways, really nice studying CS experience with him.

This teacher is very good. His lectures taught me a lot, as he uses analogies and real world examples of how we can use in our code what we learn in his class. The only downside is that there is a LOT of homework to do. Overall this guy is a great prof and I definitely recommend him to someone learning to code.

Fantastic prof, the best prof in CS so far. He is very enthusiastic in class and obviously loves teaching. He encourages questions in class by offering textbooks. He brings toys and other things to illustrate concepts concretely. He brought once a volleyball to show catching exceptions ;-) Those who do not come to class are missing a lot.

Professor Zaiane is really good at teaching-- he uses analogies and physical examples to help you understand the concepts better. I really don't like computing science but he makes computing science much more bearable. However, the course load in his class is pretty heavy, 3-4 assessments every week, even on midterm weeks but there's no final exam.

He is an excellent professor. However, he has ramped up weekly assignments for this course from previous semesters. Depending on the week, there are about 3-4 things due for this ONE class. For any non CS majors this can easily pile up and get overwhelming. 
Attendance is mandatory as he gives "in class assessments" on arbitrary, undisclosed days.

I loved his lectures! He had fun examples in class and explained everything thoroughly. Attendance was mandatory because he would call out random names in class and ask them to answer questions about the previous lecture. I think this was a 175 policy, not just his class?

best prof I've had this university by far, gives an intuitive understanding of the material. just go to his class and listen carefully, you'll do well. only ick was that he didn't allow you the chance to increase your participation grade if you came late which he said he would do at the start of the session.

Great prof. He would demonstrate concepts in class using props in the room like tables, chairs, and things he would bring in to class. I found that these examples were really helpful for understanding. Definitely go to the lectures even just for that alone and I found his explanations clear and straigtforward to understand.

Dr. Zaiane lectures in such a way that helps visualize any concept taught in the course. He uses tables, chairs, and even his own brought-in props to try to help visualize an otherwise abstract concept. He is also very willing to explain a concept in a different way. Great prof overall.

Should attend the lectures, he calls people randomly to answer questions each lecture. His lectures are amazing lol, he clearly explains everything and answers students' questions patiently. However, if he could slide in more examples and go over some practice during his lecture, it would be perfect, even though the midterms and final are not hard.

He is lecture is amazing and he explains everything clearly! I love him so much! One thing tho, is that since he talks about everything in detail, he always seems to be running behind the syllabus, however, I would highly recommend his course!!

He obviously enjoys teaching and cares about the students' learning. I loved how he used trinkets and hand made objects to illustrate concepts like adding and removing from containers (stack, queue, etc). Illustrating sorting algorithms with these toys on the table was fantastic, engaging and interesting . The videos he provided were a huge help.

An absolute phenomenal professor, his lectures are by far the best out of all three professors teaching 175 this semester and his lecture videos honestly got me through the class. He teaches concepts really well and explains the implementations line by line in an excellent way. Definitely would recommend.

Prof. Zaiane is one of the best profs I have had so far, he is clearly passionate and extremely knowledgeable about the subject matter and does his best to help students understand and succeed. 

If you come to lectures you will do well, as a bonus he also sometimes discusses real world/career applications of the topics. Excellent prof.

The GOAT, best prof I have ever had. He loves to teach and puts in so much effort through his lectures to engage the students and to make sure they are learning in the best environment possible. Can't recommend enough. In fact, I even changed my major to CS partially because of him.

He's an ok prof, he cares about attendance and he calls on people to answer questions which is ... eh. Otherwise, he really cares about the material he's teaching and in general he cares about his students. I found it difficult to concentrate in his lectures typically after the first 10 minutes... he kinda just reads code off the slides.
The amount of effort professor Zaiane put into his lecture slides made difficult concepts easy to follow and was greatly appreciated. Anyone who does not attend lectures is missing out.
The worst prof ever. Please avoid at all cost.

Pros
learn a lot 

generous grading at the start Uses best x of y system. If you did good at the start, which is possible cuz you get graded on good practise at the start, you can pass the course

Final not worth much

Cons
course structure that was changed eventually

A lot of content, some hard

No late work for ANY reason

vague-ish grade scheme

Dr. Buro is a knowledgeable person and is passionate about his field, but this does not translate to his teaching abilities. Learning C/C++ is already very difficult, but with him it is 10x moreso the case. At least 80% of the material you have to self-teach outside of class, and most labs/quizzes (and the final) were impossible to complete on time
worst class I have ever taken, even the final exam is structured very weirdly and not good. If you ask a question, he rambles on for 5 mins and you end up even not knowing what the actual answer is.

Hes a tough prof.ton of Homework.the timed labs are a nightmare. if you're taking this learn C/C++ already.take easy subject to balance the GPA. you do learn a lot of stuff, he teaches you the right things. expect to go through hell for 12 weeks but you will learn a lot , it will make you a better CS student but you have to put in a lot of work.
q

Course load was absolutely ridiculous and the class structure couldn't be worse if they tried. When they were designing this class they approached with one goal in mind, "how can we make this the most miserable, awful, and abysmal introduction to C/C++?" Somehow they managed to do it, worst class I've ever taken.
Terrible.

Honestly, if you enrol in his cmput 201 fully online, it's a guaranteed going to drop your GPA, no matter how much time you put in (unless you know C/C++ going into the course).

Advice would be to learn both C and C++ on youtube during a summer break if you want to pass his course first time.

Not a fan of the course structure...

The worst instructor I've ever met .Super unfriendly for students who have just begun learn programing and for those who are not English native speakers.Super disgusting and unreasonable course setting.If you aren't a quick learner,those time-limited quizzes and labs will destroy your grade.If he teaches CMPUT201,don't choose this course.

I really regret taking cmput 201 this semester. My gpa should not be determined by which term I decide to take a course. Buro's course was complex, covered multithreading and advanced topics. Pop quizzes were funny as well. Best of luck if you enroll in his course.
speechless...

Buro is a good prof but he expects too much of his students. At first, he weren't going over the lecture notes and just doing examples of the concepts. Towards the end of the term, he started going over the lecture notes which was nice. The time constraints and the expectations will demotivate you from learning.

This course designed by Buro is absolutely horrible. Weekly pop quizzes and timed labs. The questions on these pop quizzes/labs are given as if we've had weeks to understand the material. Labs are the most stressful part of this course, most students don't finish. Feels like Buro assumes we don't have a life/other courses that also need our time.

This is the most difficult class I've ever taken because of Buro. Weekly homework, weekly pop quizzes, weekly reading assignments, and the worst of it all is weekly timed labs. Labs does not help students practice and learn but instead is very punishing especially for those who might take longer to read and understand the task. terrible  method.

It's my first time to write something on rmp. He just assumes that you read all the reading material and understand everything well before his class. Also, the lab is timed. If you have learned everything about C and C++, maybe you can do a good job. But for most students who want to learn something and practice programming, avoid him.

This class wrecked my mental health. Self-teaching material with 2 quizzes a week, no teaching done during class, fixed grade boundaries (currently, class average is failing), and a prereq. for many upper-level CMPUT courses. Horrible experience. If you're not good at self-teaching or are very busy, absolutely wait for a different professor.

Such a bad teacher. So bad. So bad. If u need to finish CMPUT 201 and he is the only prof, do it next semester. I believe all the high-grade comments is 10 yrs ago but now is 2022. His class is only for students to review. A lot of examples without enough explanations. NEED TO WRITE 7+ CODES AND YOU CAN'T USE ANY RESOURCES. Worst prof ever.

I made an account on this website with the sole reason to tell you to never take this guys class. This class is a very heavy "learn it yourself" format. Buro does not actually teach anything. He simply links websites and PDF's for you to read and teach yourself. Everything is timed, the pop quizzes, the lab assignments. Do not take this class.

Don't take this class if he is the one teaching it. He makes everything he gets the opportunity into a test/quiz, such as having timed labs. Labs are meant to teach students how to code, not punish them with a time limit. He also gives pop quizzes. He knows his stuff, but the pop quizzes make every lecture anxious. This prof makes you hate coding.
There are 13 labs in the course, I had to do a lab during reading weeks. There is too much work and exams are too hard.

Today's lecture time is 25 mins, followed by in-class coding. 
You will be writing the C code for landing the Apollo 13 lunar module on the Moon. No arithmetic; only bitwise operations allowed. Your file should be no more than 30 lines, one statement per line. There are 10 testcases to pass. Lastly, no memory leaks or your mark will be halved.

CMPUT 201 is a difficult course but Guohui makes it much worse cause no student can understand what he's saying. He has extremely unreasonable exams and expectations. Regardless of what anyone says, in no universe is an 8/45 class average on a final exam written acceptable for any prof. Is he nice?  Yeah. Does that justify any rating above 3? No.

It doesn't matter what professor you have in this course--your grades will suffer. This is an extremely hard class that requires extreme commitment, and professor Lin can help you with that commitment. Show up to lectures and READ THE TEXTBOOK!!! As long as you're around class average, you will do fine in this course.
most difficult course in CMPUT 200s, please read textbook as much as possible and solve all the problems he mentioned in his demo program

CMPUT201 itself is very time consuming and hard, but Dr.Lin makes it worse. You might have some trouble trying to understand him in the lectures, and the in class codings in his sections is unreasonably harder than other sections. If you want to take this course, take it with Henry Tang instead.

CMPUT 201 is a very hard course with an incredibly heavy workload (labs are insane). All sections had similar averages, but I appreciated how Lin seemed to care about his class and our understanding of the material. To succeed: start labs early, go to lectures, and grind practice problems. Doing leetcode problems in C is seriously the strat.

CMPUT 201 is not really prof dependent, but rather the student's effort that decides the fate for themself in this course.  I've had friends that were in Tang's class but didn't do well. The labs are TOUGH, as it gets exponentially hard as the weeks go. I like Dr Lin, but I just can't give him a 5 because I could not understand him during lectures.

This course is very difficult, every component is extremely difficult. But that been said, there is a lot of practice, and materials for you to learn on, and bonus as well, and a huge curve since the avg is usually around 48 after everything is counted. Therefore as long as you give this course your everything, you will do well.

Insane difficulty for a 200lvl course. The avg score was so bad that even a 50 is considered good. Teach basic C programming and require you to write LeetCode questions within 25 minutes. The labs are also insane. No one is going to take this course if it isn't a prerequisite for many.

Terrible prof, take Tang if possible. An introductory C course shouldn't have a class average of 40%; if he does boost grades then it's just a testament to how badly the course is designed and how little Lin cares. If you get the whole idea of an in class coding correct but you don't pass the testcases during class, you get 2/10.

Absolute MENACE. Reviews don't lie. Guohui is knowledgeable but that doesn't mean he is qualified to teach. Decades of teaching but his English is still incomprehensible. Lecture notes are filled with questions never answered. Use Tang's notes instead. Insane 25 min weekly in-class codings. He is friendly, but if you care about your GPA, RUN AWAY!

Never seen such a bad prof ever. Teach the basics and ask you to write questions like LeetCode within 25 minutes. Give tricky questions for exams and quizzes. Very limited time for all course work and exams. Labs are insane.
Worst ever

Awful, horrible teacher. Very hard to understand, and the material he teaches is completely different from what's on the tests. You're better off skipping his lectures (as many did) and relying on the textbook to teach you instead. The class average is currently a 35%, I'm not kidding. Just an atrocious prof.
Go with henry tang if you care about your gpa and your money. this guy makes his in-class codings difficult for no reason

AVOID AT ALL COSTS. His lectures make no sense, me and my friends cannot understand anything he says. Be prepared to teach yourself everything. He also enjoys challenging his students by giving unreasonably diffcult in class coding exercies that he knows half the class will fail.
not enough time

This professor assumes everyone's a genius. He's been teaching for so long that he's completely forgotten what it's like to be a beginner. Stuff that should be covered over several lectures gets crammed into one, and the assignments are insanely hard. But you can't skip the class since it's required, unless you can choose a different professor.
No gOod, he doesn;t know what he was talking about at all!

Took CMPUT 397 with Paul Lu and really enjoyed his lectures. He dives into interesting topics and explains them well. The class was pretty relaxed and he was super helpful. You can tell he has a great passion for operating systems. Found this class much easier than other 300 level classes and will be taking 481 just because he's teaching
Dr. Lu's class is challenging, but the recorded lectures and optional EAs make it doable. Unstuck ai was a game-changer for my study prep - highly recommend checking it out!

The reason why I rate Dr. Lu four is that he provided a lot of useful resourses on eClass and really helped me learn by myself. Moreover, he recorded his lectures on Zoom and I really love this form so that I could learn in my own pace. Also, his policy on optional EAs can be helpful. But definitely, I only focused on the good points of him here...

Paul is very verbose and not concise;  he's difficult to learn coding from. if ur not a genius, which u r not btw, don't take this course. this is not for anyone who isn't far above and beyond in programming. you will self learn everything also his tests were incredibly subjective and all MC (which is ok but hard)
I never wore my seatbelt while driving to this professor's class.

I've never walked out of an exam thinking I could either get a 20% or a 90%. Yup that's Paullu's tests... multiple guessers that have 6 choices that are all right and ask you which one is more right. And also 20 multiple choices for 45% of your marks not even testing your ability as a programmer, but rather random stuff about the terminal command.
It's just an easy course if you have previous experience in programming and Linux. The professor is such a nice guy. If you missed the class it's okay. He always publishes the recording.
No attendance and lectures are not worth attending
Unnecessarily complicated course. Exams, while being multiple choice, require you to give answers that are "what do you think is the best?" Even when multiple answers are true. Avoid if possible.
If this guy is married, maybe I'm not completely hopeless.

His lectures have lots of rambling and aren't very useful. The exam questions (quizzes, midterm, final) are poorly written with unclarity and there aren't many practice resources for that. Lectures are live-streamed and recorded using fully online materials. The use of Git, GitHub, and "Morning Problems" (LeetCode style problems) is appreciated.
By far the worst professor in the CS department
Terrible Prof and rambles a lot, course is not engaging. The midterm and final felt unfair while he offers little to no practice for these tests which accounts for most of your grade.
Heavy tests
The three tags I put are exact antonyms to what he actually is. Very lecture heavy and no balance or engagement of material, just felt like i wanted to pass not actually learn the material.

He posts useful preclass video, but his in class lectures are just him rambling for a very long time. And the tests are all multiple choice questions that leave a lot of room for opinions. Tests are very hard. Also he refuses to help anyone with any windows related problem even though most of the class uses Windows.

Even though some of the material was interesting, his teaching style was boring and uninteresting. The test focused on how Paul Lu writes questions and which questions are best even if multiple answers are correct. All of the tests were multiple choice, which appears to be a good thing, but the questions were unusually specific.

I'll be very honest. Paul was horrible
1. No point attending class as all content was online and attending lecture was a waste of time
2. No point spending countless hours on coding assignments as they weren't worth anything
3. Quizzes and exam questions were horrible as they weren't objective at all-just paul perspective

BUT used git so that's ok

Paul Lu is the embodiment of delusion and ignorance mixed together. On top of 6 hours of in class time, which are a complete waste of time to attend, he releases 2-3 hours of lecture videos a week on obscure topics not designed for an introductory coding course. If you get even the slightest chance to get another prof, definitely take it....Run.

Lorna is a very good prof, she gives clear lectures and her notes are all typed and very clear. She doesn't give any homework or assignments but there are practice questions. She's very lenient on marking if you skip a quiz or midterm the weight is transferred to the final

I feel sorry to know that this is her last semester to teach because this is class is comprehensive and builts great foundations for many upcoming computer science topics. Class is easily understood and if you do the sample questions and quizzes, you'll be fine!

She's a solid prof for this course. She's not the most engaging lecturer at all and sometimes can be kind of unclear with the point she's trying to get across, but she posts all her fantastic notes on eclass, first midterm was on the tough side, but the second midterm and final were fair. Quiz questions were just variations on practice questions.
She is awesome She posts all her notes online and they are sufficient to study Definitely recommend her

Just my humble opinion. I think she is average. I did not really understand the proofs in her class and did not really learn much from her. Learn the most by reading her notes and the textbook or by doing the questions. She is one of the most organized teacher I've seen so far. She sometimes gives the lecture notes for the upcoming weeks in advance
A great prof who tried her best to keep class engaging. Well organized notes, which were posted online. Assignments and seminar questions were similar to those on the midterms and final.

Class is quite boring, but her notes (posted online) are fantastic. Speaks slowly and monotone, but knowledgeable. If you require a good lecture to learn, find another prof, but if you just need good notes take her class!
She speaks slow but very good prof, definitely recommended.

Very good prof, falls just short of what I would call a great prof. Explanations are quite good, although be sure to YouTube a lot of topics for further clarification. Not the greatest prof ever, but by no means a bad one. Recommended.
Horrible instructor. Class itself is boring, and her monotone makes going to lectures unbearable. All assignments and tests are easy, but fairly time consuming. Avoid this prof.

Friendly prof but was sometimes disorganized (programs not working, etc). Class is quite boring and the languages taught are mostly useless. Exams were not too hard and minimal studying should pull you through no problem. Easy labs that can be done at home. Didn't even read the textbook. Decent prof and easy science credit if you need one.

Seems very friendly, although I never actually went to talk to her after class. Her explanations were clear, but she didn't always seem completely prepared (a program did not work, so we spent most of class correcting it) All in all a good prof though :)

ugh what a drag both the course and the teacher. she makes me not care about the class and is very confusing as well as has illegible writing which doesn't help since all she does is write on the projector.

She is the Martha Stewart of algorithms!  Wonderful prof.  Very nice and helpful.  Writes clear and useful notes in class that closely follow the textbook; exams and assignments based directly on the notes.  Marking is very fair and she will go over exams and give back marks if there was a mistake.  Tough final but still fair.  Taken: Winter 2011.

Excellent presentation of the material. Assignments covered the material well and were easy to complete if started early. Later exams evaluated competence instead of rote memorization, which is simply outstanding. Highly recommended.
Just overall a uncaring professor. She teaches well, but has no care at all for her students. Stutters a lot, overall not a bad prof though.
fair reasonable professor

I found her lectures to be quite dry...and difficult to get motivated by. Didnt help that the course was fairly dull itself. I found her explanations to range from good to terrible depending on the circumstance and material being covered...overall didnt enjoy her teaching...but could have been in part due to the course.
She was a great prof. She made the concepts in 304 seem easy! A wonderful prof! The lectures were enjoyable to go to (that's saying a lot for a theory course:) )!
This course could have been a nightmare, but Dr. Steward had great teaching abilities.

This class might be a bit boring, with lots of content to memorize, and the assignments can be a bit hard, but it's very easy to get a good grade. The professor is very nice, and I really enjoy listening to his lectures.

This course required much more ML and search knowledge than the prereqs. 355 or 366 should be taken before. The lectures were vague and unhelpful, covering many complex topics with not enough detail and visual examples. The final and midterm were both written in a really nitpicky, memorization-heavy way. Stuff the cheatsheet as much as possible.

Seems like a nice guy. His lectures were pretty dry and basically just huge walls of text - I feel like a few more in-class examples would have helped. The ML part of the course was hard to follow without any background. I found 2/4 assignments super easy, but the other 2 took forever and I still ended up doing poorly - but YMMV. Okay 4xx course.

His slides are knowledge dumping. He teaches many materials that people would usually take a whole semester to learn in week and expect students to understand it like an expert.

He does not give a damn about his students. His exams are nit-picky. He is the reason why many students suffer and lose their passion for this industry.
Best professor for cmput 204, make the course easy to follow and detailed explanation for each algorithm
This was for CMPUT 204
Great prof

As most CMPUT courses, this course was also self-taught. Assignments and quizzes are very fair. easy to get good marks. Midterm and final are mostly trivia questions. It's either you know it or you don't know at all. Overall the course was easier than other CMPUT courses.

This is a warm reminder: if this is your only 400-level course and you need a reference letter for your graduate school application, you should never expect one from him  (even got an a/a- in this class, lol).

Wasn't expecting the A+, but I guess he was generous with the curving. I thought some of the assignments were pretty fun. Even though I'd previously completed CMPUT 355, I still found the midterm to be tricky. Class average for the final was ~66%, so be sure to study hard (and fill up your 'cheatsheet').

This is for CMPUT 204. His lectures were decent but his testing was the best. Quizzes, midterms, and the final all felt really fair. Not easy of course since this is 204, but if you do your studying on the basics of how each algorithm works and its runtime you should do good, which is better than most 204 profs at the UofA.

(for cmput 204)

Based Prof.

Terrible grading setting with very boring lectures. His powerpoints have about 50-60 pages each (* 26) with confusing logic and massive amounts of topics. 
You MUST remember 11 papers for taking the closed-book final exam...
ANYWAY, THIS IS NOT A EASY COURSE. It can be your GPA killer.
Assignment marks are completely random for assignment 4. Horrible professor with terrible marking schemes.
LoL literally marked the assignments incorrectly for half the class, was rude and refuses to apologize.
Such irrelevant and subjective questions.
Tests are unfair
Good

I have emailed the faculty to complain because this is unfair, I received multiple rude emails from him during the course while asking for help. DO NOT TAKE THIS COURSE WITH HIM. He should not be teaching for a university. He clearly does not care about her students, absolute waste of time and money.
Waste of time. Don't take his classes. All the test questions are 'who said what in which article'. Extremely irrelevant questions.
what an old trush!

Avoid Mario's CS5200 course. With 50% of the grade depending on two mid-term exams, luck matters more than effort. Choosing Mario (if other options exist) is unwise. Exams are entirely multiple-choice, often unrelated to course material or practical database use. Success depends on luck, leading to varied grades that can heavily impact GPA.
This professor is amazing, He is pretty chill as a prof. Thats all because I dont have words for this guy.Also the guy who said he punishes for answering wrong is a liar too. Good day my friends.
Never think of taking him, TA can't even open .rar file, prof doesn't listen to students. Overall awful course, and if you know python already, and have taken stat 151, you'll learn nothing.
Wouldn’t  recommend cmput191 to anyone, this whole course doesn't make any sense.

He is ok. Too strict when he does not have to be, a bit naive and is not the best when it comes to posting on eclass. 191 was kinda interesting but everyone struggled in the labs until I convinced him to change the auto grader to give expected output
Can't learn anything on his lectures, eclass is a mess, he often forgets to post new pre-class videos or post wrong or repeated pre-class videos, anyway don't take his class.

He'll change the grading criteria to make u pay for others' mistakes If you're supposed to do 25% of the work as a member in a group of 4, but someone didn't do their part & you got the contribution percantage as (50,40,10,0), the person with 40% is still gonna lose a lot of marks cuz there is someone did 50% even though 40% is already extra work!!
I think he can be a good prof if he's more willing to listen to feedback.

Avoid Mario. This class was incredibly mismanaged, the grading schemes are ridiculously harsh, and assignment instructions were terribly unclear. He seems like a nice enough guy in person but some of the ways the course was managed is inexcusable.

Mario is a really good prof, who goes an extra mile to make students comfortable with different things such as pairing for group projects, no participation form group members etc. His lectures are very interactive and I have honestly started to enjoy database stuff because of this class. Assignments are fairly simple if you keep up with lectures

The course material is very interesting, and his teaching is alright, but the course is managed horribly, group assignments suck, he gives us a week to do large assignments, and he took 2 months to correct coursework, we got our coursework grades 2 weeks before exams. Avoid.

AVOID HIM!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! Lectures suck, can't explain anything. Asgmts wording are confusing, even native speakers can't understand what do those questions mean. Always change something before deadline, I rewrote my asgmt4 three times due to that. But the difficulty of this course is fine, I got almost full marks on everything before final.
Pretty bad at explaining concepts and is not clear at times. He can sometimes get distracted and it's hard to follow what is being said.
Left assistant prof to teach. Assitant prof did not know what he was talking about
No partial mark for 99%-correct answer of quizzes. Unclear marking schema of group projects.

He is a good prof and takes the time to make sure students understand concepts. Although sometimes I think he spends too much time on easy subjects, often falling behind schedule. As long as you pay attention and read the slides, the course is easy. The assignments are also very practical. Contrary to what he says, the textbook is not necessary.
Very friendly guy.  I really liked the assignments, very practical database stuff.  The specifications were always a little bit murky with lots of clarifications added to the discussion board later.
Great prof.  He really enjoys the subject and tries to make sure you actually learn the theory behind programming.  I can't recommend him enough for anyone who actually enjoys Computing Science!
Not very helpful in or outside of class.
A nice person

Good lecturer but will go on tangents, and the course felt disorganized. We did worksheets almost every class, yet the tests didn't really match what we practiced. When our group had problems, we didn't get much support and were told to handle it ourselves. An otherwise simple class ended up feeling unnecessarily difficult.
He is a good prof, exams are fairly easy if you just do the in class exercise.Just get a good group for the team project and this class should be a easy A
Would not recommend; unclear course rules. It is a shame, as he does teach well, but he is very unspecific on important rules. A little too strict on some parts, inconsistent is the word
In a department that's terrible, this guy shines. But if you compare him to literally any other department, he's mid. CS really is just that bad.
Worst prof to ever exist! Stop picking on student during class! As someone with anxiety it makes me not want to come to class! Horrible

Hindle is just an excellent prof, start to finish. He has actual industry experience, excellent slides, and is a fantastic lecturer. Regular in-class worksheets for participation marks so don't skip class. Apart from that find yourself a good group for the project and you'll be all set! The class is a ton of work, but also totally extremely useful.
The most kind hearted professor I have had. Very understanding, but fair. Class participation matters. Knowledgeable, patient, and good at teaching!
He's the Midterm Wizard **.  Prof Hindle is such a great teacher, he cares about the students and try his hardest to make everyone understand in the lecture. I would 100% recommend him.

Hindle was a great lecturer who made a lot of the important topics in this course very interesting and digestible. Showing up to class and doing the participation exercises is a great way to guarantee success on the midterms and in the course. As for the group project, that largely depends on the quality of your teammates, but get started early!
Horrible and extremely rude with vague assignment descriptions.

I had him for both 404 and 301 and he was great for both. You can tell he's extremely knowledgeable about the topics he teaches and he's fantastic at explaining concepts. He's a bit intimidating and isn't the warmest prof out there but he was never once rude. Exams in both 404 and 301 both had a pretty annoying structure though.

He was a really good prof. The lectures were engaging - he used Twitch and Google spaces to answer student questions. There were in-class participation exercises and he did a superb job of interacting with students during lectures. His class was a lot of work: a large group project, some sole assignments, lab assignments, and 3 midterms.
He interrupted our final project presentation when we still had more time. It is disrespectful to me and my teammates : (

A real treat. 
His humor is subtle but hilarious. 
He is superbly knowledgeable, and does not mind re-explaining things.
He is a pleasure to converse with in email, and very understanding. 
Thank you Hindle.

Well spoken, never hesitates to repeat something, always answers questions when students ask. Professor Hindle gives very good advice when it comes to assignments, user stories, and industry advice (even for pursuing grad school!). The online format allowed me to keep my job while attending his class, though at times it may get busy, i learned alot

By far the best professor of my university career. Very well organized, conveys topics clearly and always tries to make the lectures enjoyable. Though fair, the exams were fast paced. Students must understand the material before hand instead of giving them time to sift through notes mid exam. Also, his twitch channel is truly something special.

Great prof that explains concepts well and made lectures enjoyable to watch. I felt midterms only tested mastery in concepts due to harsh time constraint so make sure you really understand material and can work fast. Great prof overall but be ready to put lots of time into the course.

Hindle is one of the more passionate profs in Computing Science at the U of A; however that does not mean he has no shortcoming. His exams were nothing like the practice material, he answered all questions during class with a condescending tone, and expects a lot out of you. Yet, he does care, will respond quickly, and will teach you lots.

If you have read horror stories about toxic bosses and wanted to get a glimpse of how it feels to experience that, then just take a course of this prof. Not sure how people like this are allowed to teach, much less paid for teaching.

The class is genuinely so bad, she cannot properly give a lecture. She just reads off slides and goes on random tangents about things. the group project deadlines are horrendous and the marking scheme is weird. do not take this class with her
Do not take this course if you love your sanity
not helpful at all, won't even give you 1 extra submission even if you let them know within hours. Unless dire necessity do not take her.

VERY FUN and AWESOME lectures, not sure why some people dont like this course or the prof.
The course itself is a bit challenging, but you can learn a lot from it.
Fantastic prof! 10/10 Really kind and caring person!! Lectures and teaching styles utilized are superb!!

Organization of the canvas site was nonexistent, in fact it was unfinished. It was a long unorganized page of links, some were expired or broken and there were duplicates that lead to different pages. Dropped because I didn't want to deal with that for an entire semester. Has a generally negative reputation and personally hasn't been accommodating.

Random in class marked exercises, but mostly just for credit. Was sometimes not very kind to students asking questions. Overall though lectures were informative, and marking was fair. Random criteria added to assignments midway without online notifications.

The only thing wrong really is just some of the takes she has regarding technology. Otherwise, really the only problem I've seen people have is just that she's tough when grading. But if you read the rubric you're fine. Her participation exercises kinda are annoying, but they help to check if I need to watch the last lecture or not. Good prof.

Hard course. There was no answer key for the practice finals, and I still don't know my grade even though the new semester started a week ago,, but the lectures were not too bad and were easy to follow. Lectures and in-class exercises were useful for preparing the final. No midterms, so it's manageable if you have a good group for the project.

Professor Campbell is very underrated in terms of knowledge and helpfulness. She definitely knows what she is teaching, is very responsive on forum and does practice exercises in class which helps prepare for the finals, also there was no midterm.
I am so tired of these dramas, final only, no midterms grade are highly concentrated in finals and the group project, and she often spend 15-20 minutes to do the quiz in every lecture

I think Dr. Campbell is a great lecturer. She is clearly experienced and connects concepts to practical, funny, and memorable examples. She is very nerdy; sometimes she pauses awkwardly or makes jokes that don't land, but she makes an effort to be fun.

She raised her voice at our section once early on, but has been very friendly since then.
Mean and disrespectful !

Hazel is good at lecturing, but her reactions to disruptions are intense, to the point where I constantly feel on edge. It's as if I'm walking on eggshells every time I step into the classroom, terrified that one small mistake, like my phone accidentally going off, could lead to a public scolding. I have never felt so unsafe in a classroom.

I come to the class for the drama, at the expense of other students' learning of course , but the lecture script would totally pass for a broadway show. But at the same time, she makes me wanna drop this course

The Professor is not respectful towards students - I saw it happening. As a student with at least 4 years of post-secondary education, I agree with most Professors: "No question [related to the class and current topic] is dumb, please ask".
Great professor
I didn't dins them to be helpful at all. They weren't ready to cooperate regarding some assignment extension though I had a valid reason.
By the way, the avg grade for her 400-level course is 2.6. You know what, you are killing students' future, Dr. Campbell.

Zac is based. Insanely smart guy, and watching him solve Kattis problems in class was an experience in itself. He is always active on discord, and down to answer any questions. Very approachable and kind human. CMPUT 303 is one of the best courses I've taken in university, and I highly recommend taking it with Zac if it's offered.
He was a father to me. A watchful protector of the CS department. His brain spams millions on galaxies. His kattis rank is enough to amuse you. Would recommend. But the course load was a lot tbh.

He was very kind: accepting my correct-in-spirit python TLE solutions for marks and only docked 2% on my final project for not including any testing whatsoever. I didn't get more than 50% on more than half the weeks problem sets but still ended with C, expected minimum pass. In 274/275 he wasn't as accommodating due to class size logistics

This is a graduate theory course requiring background in linear algebra and undergraduate courses of algorithms. You have to prove theories in the assignments. Overall the course helps you have a solid foundation at the polynomial-solvable algorithms and importantly, you learn to prove! Among 10 students, I got A- with an average of 84%.
Amazing CMPUT 403. Definitely recommend him.

Took this course online during the pandemic. He was not a very merciful or understanding professor. There were two large assignments and a smaller assignment due every single week, which took me over 14 hours to complete. When I mistook a deadline by a single day, he was merciless to accept the completed assignment, which was worth 10% of my grade.

Good professor. Will challenge you but IMO that's the best way to grow. I see these other reviews say he sometimes goes too fast in lectures. If that's happening just raise your hand and ask him to slow down or explain a concept better, and he'll be happy to do it. He's a very nice person and legitimately my favorite professor at UAlberta.
The professor speaks extremely fast and its difficult to comprehend what he is coding as he changes the code very fast too

Nice if I had questions but the course feels disorganized. He expects proper style on certain assignments but often reinforces poor habits at the end of every class and will provide you with code that looks like it was written by a high school student. Difficult class if you lack prior CS knowledge but covers interesting (if somewhat niche) topics.

I preferred the first part of this course that was not taught by him, however he was an okay prof. Tends to code very fast in lectures so it is impossible to keep up and code along with him. Will post a lot of his code for you to use in assignments but that doesn't really help if you don't know what it does. Nice guy and pretty approachable.

He spoke extremely fast. The mic couldn't keep up and sometimes. You'd just hear mumbling and no real words. He changed his code just as fast so you didn't have a chance to understand what he'd changed before he's compiling. He spent so much time discussing algorithms without explaining how to code in c++. I self taught myself most of this course.

He is a decent, knowledgeable professor, but he can be a little bit rude sometimes to stop people and ask things. Even another professor stopped by him several times. In my opinion, he is not the best also not the worst. Good course, TAs are really responsible.
One of my favourite profs at UofA! He really cares about the stuff hes teaching and that makes classes fun and interesting

Overall really nice prof. He does make a few mistakes here and there but he posts all his lectures and code online and is a very approachable guy. Very positive and willing to work through questions until you fully understand. Can't say that about many Computer Science profs at the University Of Alberta.
Can't teach. Reads off the slides and expects you to know everything beforehand. You're better off reading the textbook. Don't waste your time on this course with this prof.

Straightforward requirements, always kept the room open to discussion and questions and encouraged participation. The course does get a bit confusing from the middle onwards but exams were always very fair.

Instead of just reading off the slides, James opened the class up for discussion and many interesting perspectives and ideas were shared that I had not previously thought about. In other classes the professors do not care to delve into such theoretical discussions or scenarios. This gave me a framework to think about problems in upper level classes

Course was fine I guess. The slides were basically useless, but the exams and assignments were fair.

James has a habit of using his own made-up terminology sometimes which can be kind of confusing. Not bad overall though, you can tell he knows the content and cares.

Not great. The lecture slides are absolutely useless for learning. The rare examples in the slides mostly do not have full solutions and the other content is vague. There is not enough feedback for assignments either so you are left not ever knowing how to do the questions for exams. There was a lack of necessary information throughout the course.

There is no attendance requirement, but you have to go to class, because it is impossible to learn just by looking at the courseware. The assignment is difficult and always changes questions, the advantage is that you have 14 days to complete it. If you want to get a good grade in 200-level computer science, it's not 261. Don't take this class!!

This class provides a high-level overview of AI and will be tricky if it's your first exposure to it. I found the lectures insightful (although sometimes lacking concrete examples). The assignments are hard, but you get lots of time to work on them. The exams are easy. Overall, I think James Wright is a great professor.

I am not sure why this guy gets such a bad rap. I'm at the end of my 3rd year, and IMO this is one of the better taught and easier CMPUT 2XX courses. He's engaging and receptive to questions during lectures which I found refreshing. Some topics can get a bit hairy, but there's additional resources (textbooks) that he links for free.
Teaches as though he is doing revision of the concepts which has been already been taught several times before. The first two assignments coding part felt like of intermediate python rather than AI.
Not very good.

James Wright is an respected individual in the AI society. However, having knowledge about a topic does not mean you can teach it to students at a beginner level. This class is very hard to follow, with too much material. There's no practice questions, nor solutions. Not very helpful unless you're already familiar with the topics. Won't recommend.
Assignments are really hard, but very good for learning C. Lectures and Slides aren't enough to study for all of the course, the textbook is a must have for the exams.

Preface: Do not take more than one class with him in a term, the assignments have the same due dates. The class is incredibly interesting, and Ioanis makes the lectures really fun and exploratory. The assignments were very interesting and fun if you have competent partners. No final, but the three quizzes were as hard as the department is about ML.

Ioanis is a great and caring guy, who does know his way around systems development. He makes the course content interesting, even though he goes on many tangents. His 3 assignments are time consuming, but very rewarding, teaching you some rather useful skills. His tests were quite hard. If you hear "there will be multiple choice", pray.

Yannis is a nice guy but unfortunately not a great prof. The 4 "quizzes" test on the material taught in class, unfortunately what he teaches in class and the slides are not enough, requiring you to resort to Google and the textbook to really learn the material in order to pass the quizzes. However, the assignments worked on in the labs are good.
Ioanis can ramble off topic a bit but when he gets down to the material it's usually clear what he means and he's very thorough on what the important topics are to know in general and for the tests.

He's quite an interesting prof, but his lectures are mostly just him rambling. If you read his notes and the textbook you should be fine for the quizzes. His "quizzes" are like midterms, and he doesn't provide any practice questions. The assignments are interesting and fun, but they require you to do a bit of research and reading.

The prof made this so much more difficult in my opinion. It's hard to follow what he is talking about in class and can sometimes be a bit insensitive to students. Very bright person but not the best at teaching. Assignments are fair but their descriptions makes them really hard to understand. Be sure you ask the TAs what they want.

He used another profs course materials and seemed to care little for lecturing. Student questions were usually answered condescendingly. Maybe he's used to teaching higher-level courses. The course itself is difficult and he often skims over difficult concepts. You'll learn a lot if you can keep up, but you're going to have to work for it.

Yannis is quite the character. His lectures tend to be of the rambling sort, but he randomly says funny stuff, so sometimes it's worth the entertainment value alone. His writing leaves something to be desired, but he knows his stuff. Beware of his 'quizzes', which are essentially a class-long midterm of the long answer type.

Nice prof and class, grading was fair and expectations were clear. The projects and assignments were fun and helped my learning a lot, good AI policy too. Main complaint is the lack of prep materials for the final.
May be a furry but at least he teaches well

Nice and chill prof, but sometimes he does not really explain something clearly, so you really have to ask him questions for clarification. Be prepared for 2 big assignments (the 2nd SQL assignment took me lots of time), and 2 group projects (Sqlite in Python and mangoDB) that were interesting but time-consuming. The final was fair lol

Assignments and mini-projects were really fun and easy. Probably the most enjoyable part of this class. I wish he gave more practice material for the midterm and final though. He mostly read off the slides but sometimes he'll write stuff down during the lecture, so you should probably attend class. Mid prof tbh.

Professor Rafiei's exams are extremely fair.

The group projects (choose your own group) provide a valuable learning experience and have extra credit.

The quizzes biweekly, low-stakes, open-book and a great way to test.

Lab sessions require participation but are easy.

Lectures are somewhat unorganized after the midterm and require extra reading.

I think Davood is actually a pretty good prof and most of the hate is for the course. The course is pretty difficult and it is quite unique compared to other CS courses meaning you probably need to put in more time to understand concepts compared to other courses. Exams and assignments were pretty fair in terms of material but on the harder side.:)
The notes were really bad after the midterm. There were some bonus marks for group projects and the scaling was very nice.

I think Davood is overhated for no reason. He does have a few shortcomings, but most profs/people do. He does a LOT of examples in class which I found to be very helpful. Also this semester's final exam was very fair. However beware of his midterms.

Wasn't great at teaching, so its not worth going to lectures. The exams are difficult due to the way they are graded. Assignment 2 was unecessarily difficult, however the group projects are fine if you're in a good group.

Of my three years of uni, this is the first prof I would firmly state that delivers their course terribly what so ever. Gives answers by repeating the question on the test, does not take feedback, twists the words of negative feedback into as words of good feedback of him, textbook is better at teaching but has some unnecessary topics.
He does not prepare for the exam at all

The exams are so so difficult than what he teaches during the lectures. As if he teaches us how to wash rice and asks in exam how to make fried rice. Also, his lectures are not very clear, and too much of theory and very less practice/examples for the concepts.

The dude reads off the slides so there is no value to coming to class

Assignment 1 was easy 2 was dramatically different in how hard it is START EARLY

The two group projects are ok if you have decent people

Midterm was Hard due to the lack of time you have to do it

The Prof him self really could care less about his students.
I do not think he cares a bit about the students. His notes and lectures are terrible and will never prepare you for an exam or an assignment. You will have to self learn everything from youtube.
his lecture is well structured, exams are easy, but some times he doesn't explain things well

Ignore mistakes students pointed out and 50% of questions in class are unsure. Waste most of the time teaching something highly related to assign proj. Teaches in an extremely unintuitive way

Teaches 2+2 = 4 and expects calculus in assignments and exams
Very tough grader
a murder
Legend.

The assignments/mini-projects were the hardest part in this class. Mostly TA's are supposed to help, but he tells TA's to not help. If you have questions about these projects, you cannot email TA's or him personally, they have to be posted on the discussion forum. Discord helped more than any TA or the professor himself.

He is a really great prof. He explains everything, responds to emails and cares about students. His explantion of the material is clear, and exams are not hard. He always tries to find new interesting approaches.

His lectures will help a ton with the lab component. Only the odd time it will help with a test. Learn the fundamentals from another resource like YouTube and you will ace the quizzes. He is very good at programming and understands what he teaches. Very nice through email. Has a accent. If he is an option then definitely worth considering.

Professor Sander teaches the material thoroughly and occasionally interacts with the chat, answering questions in the chat and asking us a few problems. He leaves a few minutes after each class to discuss with him and he responds timely on emails. If you don't have previous programming experience, it'll be tough if you don't pay attention.
Put in the work, and this course will not be bad, in fact, you may enjoy it it quite well. Sanders is generally helpful over email, and his explanations are quite good.

Worst prof I have ever had, he is absolutely no help and expects you to know stuff before you learn it. Everyone failed the first quiz, and probably isnt doing well now. When you ask questions he answers with Rewatch the videos Read what I wrote and doesnt ever help you.

Very interested in seeing his students do well in the course and will provide help outside of class if necessary. No attendance or participation marks so if you don't need to go to the lectures, there is nothing stopping you. Still does a good job teaching in the lectures and is good at explaining introductory concepts concisely yet accurately.

I found his lectures very dry and started going to a different prof halfway through the term; that made a real positive difference. It's important to pay attention in lectures for this course and he just couldn't hold my attention.

Had close to zero coding experience prior to course. Quite difficult and don't get me started on the vignettes... Big fan of Joerg's teaching style. Really engages the class; rather than blasting through solutions to the code he will stop and get the class to solve each problem. Really loved those eureka moments by solving the problems together.

Please, if you have PRIOR PROGRAMMING EXPERIENCE, please choose Joerg to be your professor. He is so helpful and unlike other professors who would go on and on, his lecture is more like watching an experienced programmer at work! He EVEN came to my lab session at 6 pm to help me with unsolved problems! I am so touched by his kindness!
Great professor. Always willing to answer questions and very good at explaining concepts.

Joerg is an amazing lecturer. He is very caring towards the students and you can come by his office at any time. Made both the midterm and the final very clear. Only concepts that were taught in class were in the tests. The course itself might be a little scary at first but I think its designed so we grasp programming and python in the end.

His lectures are aimed towards people who had no prior experience in programming. It can be boring at times, but it is really thorough. I highly recommend that you code along with him, and try to interact with him. He's really nice.

Lectures consist of watching him write code for a game. I did not know any programming before his class, and I feel like I learned a lot. He really tries to make sure people understand. The midterm and final were really easy if you understand the basic. He has a slight accent, but he is easy to understand.

He's a good lecturer but the course design itself seems flawed. If you have no coding experience the first few weeks are going to be very difficult, as he lectures as though you have a coding background. Then in the latter half of the course, the course becomes boring and repetitive with little new content.

He is an amazing choice of professor. Open to any questions during class time and will explain it in a way that everyone understands. His midterm was very straightforward, he said he was going to make the final harder but it felt easier. There wasn't a single trick question. Every method to getting a solution of his tests were introduced in class.

He is very caring and nice. He goes through everything slowly, and always makes sure that everyone understand everything before moving one new stuff. As long as you understand the materials, you will do fine on the midterm and final, which are based on what you learned in class, no trick questions.

He is absolutely a smart person in his field. If you have computing background or already know something about programming, he will be your best choice. However, if you are a person who knows nothing about computing (like me), he will be your nightmare. He assumes you know all the python concepts before attending any courses.

Excellent prof, I learned a lot from his lectures. I did not have prior programming experience but his explanations were so clear and detailed that I was fine. The hard part of this course (and other CS courses in my opinion) is the labs. I would recommend working on them in advance and seeking help when you need it.

Honestly, this guy is ridiculously smart and knows a lot about his field. So if you already know a lot about computer science/coding then he's a solid choice. However, if you don't know much then you'll have a REALLY hard time learning anything from his lectures because try as he might he can't dumb it down from his level.

A gifted lecturer. Articulate and clear in his explanations of concepts. He answers questions on the discussion forum in a incredibly timely manner. I have not been to his office hours, so I can not speak for how he is one-to-one. Overall, he imparted in me a curiosity to continue my education in databases. So, that gets 5 stars from me.

Always cheerful and encouraging when interacting. The day I meet her for the first time, she made me feel like I know a lot and she is less knowledgeable than me, that thing made me so comfortable to start my work. But with time I realised that she is the unbeatable super hero of her field. She is a role model for me no matter where I go in life.
Lots of online readings, but exam is ok. Prof is fine, but sometimes I don't feel respected.

She is not a good supervisor. She is not so knowledgable in the field but expects you to do advanced research. When you do your research job by yourself, she interferes and complains about many things. Although some of these things are not bad, in total it bothers a lot. She is more of an investigator and auditor than an inspirational supervisor.
Eleni was a good professor overall. Do your work, do your readings and you'll do well in her course.

This prof is awful. Her clarity is sooooo bad. Accent is OK. She looks like very busy and dose not pay a lot of attention on teaching. She did prepare tons of materials for the class but few ppl want to read them cuz you can actually google those materials easily and she just put those links together. She is always ready to help, she said, only.
I learned a lot from her.
She is one of the best, If you really want to learn something, I highly recommend her , But if you want to only pass the course do not take her courses.

Nice person but terrible teacher. All questions so ambiguous with little clarification. Quiz questions she's searching for her keywords only. Exam questions unclear. Overall she asks terrible questions then doesn't help to resolve them.

Her English is good, but you need some time to get use to her accent. She knows what she is talking about, but just lacks this persona of a teacher because she almost seems mad at students for asking questions at times, and is always pretty sharp. No textbook to this course, and the entire outline is a mess. Wait for another prof if you can

One of the worst instructors I encountered while in undergrad. I could barely understand her accent, and she was not a supportive instructor. I'm not good with the subject she teaches and had a horrible time in her class. Unfortunately she is the newest iCORE Research Chair. Just because you are smart does not mean you can teach.

She had an inexplicable hatred towards me. Eleni is a very good professor.  As long as you keep up with classes and homework you will do fine.  She is a tough marker on exams (you need to know what you're talking about), but you can still come out with an A if you do well on your assignments.

This class is invaluable. Highly recommended. I learned a lot in this class (though mostly learned on my own). For the most part, your mark in this class will be contingent on how much work you put into your course project. The prof is OK. She marks fairly easily, but her lectures are fairly boring. She tends to over-explain things.

The classes were a bore but now that I'm in the workforce I find that they came in VERY handy... Everyone thinks programming is just coding, but it isnt. Know your UML, know your code smells, know how to refactor, know how to use Patterns.... thats the diff between a Nait code monkey and a UofA Developer.

Dr. Stroulia is very nice and helpful! She values your appointment and answers your questions very patiently. In her class, I learned a lot, which are pretty useful for my current internship job. I like her.

She was an okay prof.  Not as bad as some of these comments make it out to be.  Initially, I thought the entire reason that I couldn't focus in class, was because the effort of understanding her was too much, but when another prof (perfect english) lectured, he only held my attention for a few mintues more.  Make cheat sheets thorough for exams.
Too unfair. She does not know how to be a professor, even she does not know how to be a person. Worst professor i ever seen.

Poor Lectures, Fails miserably in trying to connect with students.  Plus she almost ran me over in the crosswalk on the way to class!

he is a good prof but the class itself is quite hard. the lectures aren't worth going to, you can read everything that he provides on the slides and do well in the class.

Exceptional teaching and professionalism during lectures. There is a lot of content for this class but the professor has great delivery and always is able to answer questions. Midterm and final were very reasonable if you pay attention and study the material.
Omid explained the complext content in the course well. Allow the students two EA's in the term that would transfer the weight of any project/assignment or midterm to the final.

Omid is the best cmput prof currently at UofA. I took his course online in Winter 2021 and I felt really engaged in C++ programming learning! The overall workload is somewhat heavy but trust me be work hard and you'll find everything he taught was extremely useful when coming to the career area. The final product of Assignment is wonderful
Omid was an okay lecturer and his lectures can be dull at times. He also likes to give out paper handouts during class time. His lecture notes are very good

I have taken a few classes with Omid, and the consensus from many of the students is that while being very intelligent, he is not necessarily the best lecturer (dry). He is very willing to accommodate students, and definitely knows how to explain things quite well. His slides are very good IMO. Really good choice for systems courses. Would recomend

Overall really great class to take! Adam does a great job covering a complicated topic in a way that students across faculties can understand. Final is a large portion of the grade (~45%), but the questions are all reasonable if you attend class. Assignments were really interesting to learn from, but should be worth slightly more in my opinion.

Great class for those interested in learning more about the pros and cons of AI and it's relevance in our society. The assignments are pretty straight forward but do require you to pay close attention to details.  Final is worth 45% so if you don't study early enough the final will tank your mark by quite a bit

He was amazing! Very quick at responding to emails (within a couple hours at most). I had a conflict with another final exam and he moved my exam accordingly. 

As long as you pay attention in class and review your notes before hand, you'll do great in the class. He did a great review for the final exam that made sure you'd excel if you attended.
Personally would highly recommend as I had to get a deferred final exam and he was super flexible and accommodating and did everything he could to help me.
Worth taken for his grad course RL1

Cool guy, teach clearly but too fast. Another thing I don't like him is that he does not round your final marks. You have to read the required materiel before class so that you maybe understand what he is talking about. Slides may not helpful. Do not curve. Highly recommend to watch video of David Silver instead of attending class.

Lectures are easy to follow, goes through material with sufficient detail, walks you through all the algorithms. He has a weird sense of humor and is extremely passionate about what he teaches. awesome guy

Almost weekly assignments took lots of time for few marks. Weekly reading assignments with writing required. Her assignments are good and practical, but however, the quizzes and exams format are ridiculous. 1. Class was a complete waste of time and money, learned nothing

I don't know what all the bad reviews are talking about and how the people in this department became so damn weak and lazy. Martha is one of the sweetest and nicest professors that I have ever had the pleasure of having.
